name: Downeast Blueberry Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: 1 pint fresh blueberries, sorted and stemmed
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the sugar, cornstarch, and sweetener in a bowl, pressing out all lumps.
2. Place the blueberries in a 6-cup shallow casserole dish that has been lightly sprayed with nonstick vegetable cooking spray.
3. Add the sugar mixture, lemon juice, and toss well to mix. Using a potato masher, lightly mash the blueberries. Set aside.
4. Butter one side of the bread slices with margarine. Stack the slices and cut into ½-inch cubes.
5. Add the cubed bread to the casserole dish and toss well to mix.
6. Bake the pudding uncovered in a moderate oven at 350°F for 30 minutes.
7. Remove from the oven, add the evaporated skim milk, and stir well to mix.
8. Return to the oven and bake uncovered for an additional 15 minutes.
9. Serve warm, topped with a little whole milk if desired. Makes 6 servings.
